item.title: A Thames Find with Hidden History
item.description: Nicola White explores the Thames foreshore and shares the story behind a small historical find. A mudlarking permit from the Port of London Authority is required in order to mudlark on the River

item.title: Why 19th Century Poison Bottles Were Blue
item.description: Exploring why 19th-century poison bottles were made bright blue, with insights into design and safety from the past. A mudlarking permit from the Port of London Authority is required in order to mudlark on the River Thames

item.description: Exploring a remarkable Thames foreshore find and the story it may hold. A mudlarking permit from the Port of London Authority is required in order to mudlark on the River Thames.

item.description: Nicola White explores the Thames foreshore and shares a historical find revealed by the mud. A mudlarking permit from the Port of London Authority is required in order to mudlark on the River Thames

item.title: A Remarkable Pipe Find on the Thames
item.description: Nicola White explores a notable pipe find on the Thames foreshore, where small objects reveal everyday history and the stories they carry. A mudlarking permit from the Port of London Authority is required in order to mudlark on the River Thames

item.title: A padlock with a World War One story
item.description: Nicola White explores a padlock linked to a family story from World War One, showing how small finds on the Thames can reveal personal history. A mudlarking permit from the Port of London Authority
